title: [lichen_l2.c:1103] Missing secure_zero on net_pkt alloc failure
description:
Gap analysis: Round 1 focused on ctx modifications but missed error paths after line 1081.

BUG: If net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer() fails at line 1098-1100, the function returns at line 1103-1104 WITHOUT calling secure_zero(rx_link_key, sizeof(rx_link_key)).

rx_link_key contains a copy of the session link key (line 1027-1031). When the function returns without zeroing, this sensitive key material remains on the stack until overwritten by subsequent function calls. An attacker with memory access (e.g., via a separate vulnerability, JTAG, or cold boot attack) could recover the key.

The fix is to add secure_zero(rx_link_key, sizeof(rx_link_key)) before the return at line 1104.

Similar issue exists at line 1112 in the net_pkt_write failure path.
close_reason: Not a bug: rx_link_key is already zeroed at line 1120 BEFORE the code reaches net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer at line 1137. The secure_zero call at line 1120 executes unconditionally in the success path before the #else directive, ensuring the key is cleared before any code after #endif runs. The alloc failure path at lines 1141-1144 cannot leave rx_link_key unzeroed because the key was already zeroed.
